content:
How to Apply to University of Pretoria (UP)

Thinking about studying at University of Pretoria (UP)? Based in Pretoria, Gauteng, UP / Tuks is a Traditional University established in 1908. This guide covers everything you need to know to apply successfully — from APS requirements to application deadlines, bursaries, and step-by-step instructions.

At a Glance: UP / Tuks Fast Facts

DetailInformation
Full NameUniversity of Pretoria (UP)
TypeTraditional University
LocationHatfield, Pretoria
Year Founded1908
Application Portalhttps://www.up.ac.za/applicant
Application FeeR300
Applications OpenMarch 1
Applications CloseJune 30 (some faculties close earlier)
Minimum APS28 (varies by programme)
Contact Phone012 420 3111
Contact Emailcsc@up.ac.za

Why Choose UP / Tuks?

Top 5 SA universities; QS Africa Top 10.

  • Only university in sub-Saharan Africa offering a full BVSc (Veterinary Science) degree
  • South Africa's largest residential university with 50,000+ students
  • UP's Gordon Institute of Business Science (GIBS) is rated among Africa's top business schools
  • Home to the Telkom Innovation Hub on campus

Faculties and Programmes at UP / Tuks

UP / Tuks offers qualifications across 9 faculties:

  • Economic & Management Sciences
  • Education
  • Engineering, Built Environment & IT
  • Health Sciences
  • Humanities
  • Law
  • Natural & Agricultural Sciences
  • Theology & Religion
  • Veterinary Science

Popular programmes include: BVSc (Veterinary Science), BCom, BSc Computer Science, LLB, BEng.

APS Requirements for UP / Tuks

The minimum Admission Point Score (APS) required at UP / Tuks is 28 points. However, individual programmes may require significantly higher scores:

APS range: 28–44 depending on faculty

UP requires a minimum APS of 28 for most programmes. Veterinary Science requires 34+. Medicine requires 36+. The university uses the Senior Certificate/NSC marks directly.

How to Calculate Your APS

Your APS is calculated from your Grade 12 (NSC) subject results. Each subject receives a score from 1 to 7 based on your percentage:

Percentage (%)Rating CodeAPS Points
80–100%7 (Outstanding)7
70–79%6 (Meritorious)6
60–69%5 (Substantial)5
50–59%4 (Adequate)4
40–49%3 (Moderate)3
30–39%2 (Elementary)2
0–29%1 (Not Achieved)1

Your APS is the sum of your six best subjects (excluding Life Orientation, which is counted at half-value at most universities, or excluded entirely).

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score instantly.

Step-by-Step: How to Apply to UP / Tuks

Step 1: Check Programme Requirements

Before applying, confirm the specific APS requirements and subject prerequisites for your chosen programme at https://www.up.ac.za. Some programmes require specific subjects (e.g., Mathematics for Engineering, Life Sciences for Medicine).

Step 2: Gather Your Documents

You will typically need:

  • Certified copy of your ID (South African ID or passport for international students)
  • Certified copy of your Grade 11 final results (if applying before matric)
  • Certified copy of your NSC / Grade 12 results (final matric results when available)
  • Proof of residence (e.g., utility bill)
  • Completed application form (online or paper)

Step 3: Apply Online

Visit the UP / Tuks online application portal at https://www.up.ac.za/applicant. Create an account, complete the application form, upload your documents, and pay the application fee of R300.

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications for 2027 admission typically open in March 1 and close on June 30 (some faculties close earlier). Apply as early as possible — popular programmes fill up quickly, especially Medicine, Law, and Engineering.

Step 5: Accept Your Offer

Once you receive a conditional or unconditional offer from UP / Tuks, accept it within the specified timeframe (usually 2–4 weeks). You may need to pay a registration deposit to secure your place.

Step 6: Register for Your Programme

After accepting your offer, complete the formal registration process in January (or mid-year for Semester 2 programmes). Bring original and certified copies of all documents.

Application Deadlines for UP / Tuks

Missing the application deadline is one of the most common reasons students don't get into their chosen institution. Here are the key dates:

  • Applications Open: March 1
  • Application Deadline: June 30 (some faculties close earlier)
  • Results Notification: Typically November–December for the following year
  • Registration: January (Semester 1) or mid-year

Pro tip: Don't wait until the last day. Apply when applications open — some programmes are filled on a first-come, first-served basis.

Bursaries and Financial Aid at UP / Tuks

Studying at UP / Tuks can be financed through several bursaries and financial aid options:

  • NSFAS
  • UP Financial Aid
  • Nedbank Affinity Bursary
  • UP Vice-Chancellor's Achiever Award

NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme): If your combined household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year for the following academic year.

Preparing for UP / Tuks While Still in School

The best time to prepare for university is while you're still in Grade 10, 11, or 12. Here's what you can do:

  • Focus on your key subjects: Make sure you're strong in the subjects required for your chosen programme
  • Use AI tutoring: StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps you master difficult CAPS topics before matric
  • Research your options early: Visit open days at UP / Tuks to understand campus life
  • Apply for bursaries early: Some bursaries (like Allan Gray, Anglo American) are available while you're still in Grade 11
  • Track your APS: Use our APS calculator to know where you stand

What Happens After You Apply?

After submitting your application to UP / Tuks, the process typically follows these stages:

  1. Application Confirmation: You'll receive an email or SMS confirming receipt of your application
  2. Conditional Offer: If your Grade 11 results meet the requirements, you may receive a conditional offer
  3. Final Results Submission: Submit your final NSC results in January
  4. Unconditional Offer: If your matric results meet or exceed the requirements, your offer becomes unconditional
  5. Registration: Register for your programme and pay your fees or confirm NSFAS/bursary funding

Comparing UP / Tuks With Other Universities

Not sure if UP / Tuks is the right fit? Here are some things to consider:

FactorUP / TuksTypical Alternative
TypeTraditional UniversityTraditional University / UoT
LocationPretoriaVaries
Minimum APS2820–37 (varies widely)
Application FeeR300R100–R400
NSFAS EligibleYesYes (public) / No (private)

Frequently Asked Questions About Applying to UP / Tuks

Still have questions? Our FAQ section below answers the most common questions about applying to UP / Tuks.

Need extra help with your Grade 12 subjects to meet UP / Tuks's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor is FREE to start — get personalised help in Mathematics, Sciences, English, and all CAPS subjects. Most students improve their marks by 20–30% within a few weeks.

quickAnswer: To apply to University of Pretoria (UP), visit https://www.up.ac.za/applicant, pay the R300 application fee, and submit your documents before June 30 (some faculties close earlier). Minimum APS is 28 for most programmes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS to apply to UP / Tuks?
faqs.answer: The minimum APS at UP / Tuks is 28 points for most programmes. However, competitive programmes like Medicine, Law, or Engineering may require significantly higher scores (28–44 depending on faculty). Always check the specific requirements for your chosen programme on the UP / Tuks website.

faqs.question: When do applications open for UP / Tuks?
faqs.answer: Applications for UP / Tuks typically open in March 1 and close on June 30 (some faculties close earlier). We strongly recommend applying as early as possible, as popular programmes fill up quickly. Visit https://www.up.ac.za/applicant to apply online.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee for UP / Tuks?
faqs.answer: The application fee at UP / Tuks is R300. Some students may qualify for a fee waiver — check the UP / Tuks website for details. The application fee is usually non-refundable even if your application is unsuccessful.

faqs.question: Does UP / Tuks accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: Yes, UP / Tuks is an accredited NSFAS institution. If your household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding which can cover tuition, accommodation, meals, transport, and a living allowance. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to apply to UP / Tuks?
faqs.answer: For your application to UP / Tuks, you will need: a certified copy of your ID, certified copies of your Grade 11 and/or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and any programme-specific requirements (portfolio for arts, HPAT scores for health sciences, etc.). All documents should be certified within 3 months of the application date.
